title: Autonomous Research: derailment
objective: Investigate 'derailment' through physics-based simulation and probabilistic modelling to improve railway safety standards.
rationale: Topic 'derailment' was selected because it directly addresses 6 identified research gap(s) and is supported by 5 insight cluster(s) in the literature review.

questions.question: How does train speed (up to 350 km/h) affect derailment probability on curved track with moderate geometry defects?
questions.hypothesis: Derailment probability increases non-linearly with speed, with a critical threshold above which the risk becomes unacceptable.

questions.question: What axle load (up to 250 kN) causes critical Nadal quotient exceedance under combined geometry faults?
questions.hypothesis: There exists an axle-load threshold beyond which the combined effect of load and geometry faults produces deterministic derailment.

questions.question: How do track irregularity amplitudes up to 20 mm compound with vehicle speed to affect lateral wheel force?
questions.hypothesis: Track irregularity amplitude and spatial frequency interact resonantly with bogie natural frequency, producing peak lateral forces.
